methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $90M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Second Front Systems is a Series B defense and security company founded by Peter Dixon and Tyler Sweatt that has raised $90M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 firms (2 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector at equivalent or adjacent stages, with particular focus on five private peers: Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods yield materially different results. The stage-progression method, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $90M total raised as a proxy baseline, implies a valuation of $171M. In contrast, the peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of six same-sector, same-stage comparables, which stands at $1.2B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B across the peer set. This reflects valuations of $1.5B for Vannevar Labs and Dream Security, $1.4B for Harmattan AI, and $1.0B for both Castelion and Defense Unicorns.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $145M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $686M. The wide dispersion reflects structural tension between mechanistic fundraising-based valuation and market-based peer multiples in an immature, capital-intensive sector. Key uncertainties include revenue scale, customer concentration, and how Second Front's specific competitive positioning and traction map against heterogeneous peer development profiles.
